items: Dragging a clip near any edge of the timeline scroll container causes the container to scroll in that direction.
Scroll speed accelerates as the pointer moves closer to the edge (not constant speed).
External file/asset/text-tool drops trigger auto-scroll when dragged near edges during dragover.
Marquee selection extends correctly when the scroll container auto-scrolls (selection rectangle grows to encompass newly-visible clips).
Auto-scroll stops immediately when the drag/drop/selection ends (no lingering scroll).
Auto-scroll stops when the pointer moves away from the edge zone back into the center.
When the pointer is stationary at an edge, drag indicators (drop position, snap, marquee rect) continue updating as the container scrolls — no stale state.
The drop-position indicator and snap calculations remain correct during auto-scrolling (no stale position bugs).
No visible jank or performance degradation during auto-scroll (single RAF loop, no duplicate timers).

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline auto-scroll coordination: the stale-state problem is only partially fixed. Step 3 re-runs `coordinator.update(...)` during `onTick`, but it still does not re-run the clip-drag snapping and secondary-ghost logic, so `latestStart`, snap behavior, and multi-drag ghosts can still drift while the container keeps scrolling with a stationary pointer.
unresolved_flags.evidence: In the repo, `useClipDrag` does more than call `coordinator.update(...)` on move: it derives `snappedStart` via `snapDrag(...)`, stores it in `session.latestStart` / `actionDragStateRef`, and recomputes secondary ghosts after the coordinator result (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:273-330). The revised plan's Step 3 `onTick` callback only shows `coordinatorRef.current.update(...)`, so it would refresh the drop indicator but not the rest of the drag state that currently lives below that call path. The original FLAG-001 covered stale snap/drop calculations during stationary-edge scrolling, and that part is still open for clip drags.

rationale: FLAG-001 has narrowed from "no notification mechanism at all" (v1) to "the onTick callback in Step 3 should include snap/ghost logic, not just coordinator.update()" (v2). This is an executor-level detail, not a planning gap — the plan correctly identifies the onTick pattern, the right file, and the right callback wiring. The executor will read useClipDrag:273-330 during implementation and naturally extract the full post-coordinator logic (snapDrag, latestStart, ghost recomputation) into the callback. Another iteration would refine the code snippet but not change the architecture. Score is flat at 2.0 with a narrowing flag — further iteration risks churn.
signals_assessment: Iteration 2, weighted score flat at 2.0. Plan delta was 51% (substantial rewrite to add onTick pattern), now stabilizing. No recurring critiques. FLAG-001 narrowed from "missing notification mechanism" to "Step 3 callback is incomplete" — a code-level detail. FLAG-003 (scope creep on insets) resolved and removed. Preflight clean. No debt subsystem overlaps.
warnings: During Step 3 execution, the onTick callback must include the full drag-state update path from useClipDrag:273-330 (snapDrag, latestStart, actionDragStateRef, secondary ghosts) — not just coordinator.update(). Verify this during review.
If useExternalDrop doesn't have direct access to coordinator.editAreaRef, resolve via querySelector rather than adding a new prop — keep the change surface minimal.

settled_decisions.decision: Use onTick callback on createAutoScroller (fired synchronously within existing RAF tick) as the notification mechanism for all three hooks.
settled_decisions.rationale: Addresses the original FLAG-001 stale-state concern without adding a second RAF loop or scroll event listener. Settled across two iterations.

settled_decisions.decision: The scroll container for all hooks is .timeline-canvas-edit-area (single element, both axes).
settled_decisions.rationale: Confirmed by DOM inspection across both iterations.

settled_decisions.decision: No left-edge insets needed for v1. The sticky label column is inside the scroll container and getBoundingClientRect reflects the visible area.
settled_decisions.rationale: FLAG-003 resolved with DOM evidence. Can revisit if QA reveals issues.

settled_decisions.decision: Step 3 onTick callback must include the full drag-state update path (snap, latestStart, ghosts), not just coordinator.update().
settled_decisions.rationale: Accepted as executor guidance from FLAG-001. Review should verify this is complete.

orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea: Add auto-scroll to the timeline when dragging clips or external items near any edge (top, bottom, left, right). Should work for internal clip drags (useClipDrag), external file/asset/text-tool drops (useExternalDrop dragover), and marquee selection (useMarqueeSelect). The scroll speed should accelerate as the pointer gets closer to the edge.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline auto-scroll coordination: the stale-state problem is only partially fixed. Step 3 re-runs `coordinator.update(...)` during `onTick`, but it still does not re-run the clip-drag snapping and secondary-ghost logic, so `latestStart`, snap behavior, and multi-drag ghosts can still drift while the container keeps scrolling with a stationary pointer.

resolved_flags.concern: Timeline auto-scroll: Scope creep: Step 5 adds a generalized `insets` API and changes left-edge semantics before the repository shows a real gutter bug.
resolved_flags.resolution: The current DOM intentionally keeps the label gutter inside the same scroll container: sticky track labels live in each row (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:149-152), the edit area itself is the overflow container (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:494-497), and the gutter is reinforced with a sticky `::before` pseudo-element (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/timeline-overrides.css:1-12). The repo already models content offset with `LABEL_WIDTH`/`TIMELINE_START_LEFT` (/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts:4-5). The user request was scrolling near any edge; introducing a generic inset system now expands scope and may change expected left-edge behavior without evidence.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 2.0 -> 2.0. Plan deltas: 51.1%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 1. Open significant flags: 1.
